title: Chapter Fifty-One: Thunderclap
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Hyeon Won-chang brushed his eyes with the back of his hand.

For one breath, Yeon-shin understood why. Then he saw the thunderclap bombs.

Twenty Blood Swordmasters pulling identical black spheres from their satchels, arms already cocking back with the smooth coordination of men who had rehearsed this a hundred times. Not sword technique. Not martial arts. They were applying combat footwork to the throw, afterimages rising from their hands like heat shimmer off summer stone.

*Insane.*

KWAAANG!

There was no time to shout a warning. He stamped the ground, Fate Defying Scripture flooding his lower meridians and waist vertebrae, and the earth cracked beneath his heel. Even avoiding the direct blast, his body flew against his will. His ears rang as though someone had struck a temple bell inside his skull.

THUD!

He hit the ground. Every bone sang with pain. He could not move.

Blood in his mouth. A burning sensation rising from his stomach -- internal damage. The kind that settled deep and stayed.

BANG! BOOM!

The earth rose like waves. The pristine elegance of the Lotus Pavilion vanished beneath dirt and dust and the groans of the wounded.

*The Blood Flame Sect's method for countering the Demon Destroying Blue Steel Hand is not martial arts. It is slaughter by any means necessary.*

They had already calculated that no direct confrontation could succeed. So they had brought bombs. Supreme thunderclap bombs triggered by inner power -- weapons that the imperial court had forbidden, weapons that only the Sichuan Tang Sect's craftsmen were supposed to possess. The Blood Flame Sect did not care about the imperial court. They had been declared evil already. What was one more crime?

*What about Won-chang? What about Shin So-bin?*

He staggered upright. Through the thick dust, a familiar energy settled beside him. Hyeon Won-chang, guarding the perimeter, his extraordinary eye technique cutting through the haze where normal sight failed.

-What about Shin So-bin?

-She went to scout Hwangbo Myeong-rin's side before they came. She must have headed to the pavilion.

Sound transmission. Hyeon Won-chang's voice carried only essentials. His demeanour had changed. The easy wit was gone. In its place -- the clipped efficiency of a man who understood exactly how close to death they stood.

Blood Swordmasters were closing the distance now, wielding both swords and thunderclap bombs. Their purpose was clear: massacre the Desolate Sect's Lightning Flash and every orthodox rising talent in reach.

Yeon-shin ran. Hyeon Won-chang ran with him.

His whole body felt like it was being torn apart. The distance to the pavilion was not far, but time stretched like a blade being drawn from its scabbard -- endlessly, agonizingly slow.

When they arrived, the dust was thinner above the water. He could see clearly.

Hwangbo Myeong-rin was at the back of the retreating group, slipping behind the family's young masters as they fled. Shin So-bin stood directly in front of her, watching for the Blood Flame Sect, her back turned.

The moment Shin So-bin's attention was caught by movement ahead, Hwangbo Myeong-rin struck.

A palm to the spine. Point-blank range. Enormous true qi already gathered before the blow landed -- a sneak attack so close and so sudden that even a Desolate Sect white-rank could not have reacted in time.

Shin So-bin's body arched like a bow and flew outward toward the lake.

"Th-that!"

Hyeon Won-chang's rage was immediate. But beside him, Yeon-shin's eyes only narrowed.

*Before counting to five, she hits the water. Distance: seven zhang. If she falls like that, it draws attention. She is conscious. Internal injury -- not deep.*

*That is enough.*

He analysed the trajectory in an instant and calculated the lightness technique distance. True qi rushed through bruised meridians -- the Supporting point below the hip, the Deep Gate point on the back of the thigh, the Bubbling Spring on the sole.

He forgot the pain.

KWAK!

Desolate Sect lightness technique. Three steps to the clearing's edge. Fourth step touched the lip of the lake. He leaped.

Shin So-bin's young face, eyes squeezed shut, rushed toward him.

*She has never been betrayed like this before. Daughter of a prestigious family. All-knowing attitude. But she has never had someone she trusted drive a palm into her spine.*

He stretched his left arm around her waist. His right hand caught the pavilion's outer railing. He spun like a leaf, the Moon Spirit Harmony Technique softening his inner power, deflecting the impact through rotation.

Tak.

The landing was gentle. It felt like a hammer blow.

*First time I have been this injured since entering the murim.*

"Shin So-bin. Footwork."

She opened her round eyes wide. Energy spread through her legs instantly -- she followed orders without hesitation, whatever her attitude. Her slim waist braced in his grip. Yeon-shin swung her.

PHEOK!

A Blood Flame Sect member rushing from the side caught her feet across his jaw and tumbled backward.

*Pursuit encirclement. Not just Blood Swordmasters. A Heaven-Spanning Net forming from the south.*

"Thank you -- Senior! Are you alright?"

Her thin eyebrows rose high. Yeon-shin shook his head.

"Let's go."

He rejected her hand. They needed to leave the Lotus Pavilion. Now.

---

They jumped together and landed in the chaos of the battlefield. Hyeon Won-chang found them instantly.

"They're coming up from the south."

"Northwest. Along the waterside."

"Could be a trap?" Shin So-bin said. She was rubbing her waist where his arm had gripped her, staring directly into his eyes as though daring the moment to be anything other than what it was.

He almost smiled despite the agony splitting his body.

"No choice. The lake forces two options."

"Let's go."

The three of them ran along the lakeshore. Not mindless flight. Yeon-shin was following Hwangbo Myeong-rin's trail -- the direction she had fled with her brothers and the family disciples.

*The Blood Flame Sect is a common obstacle. The mission is priority. I cannot lose the Hwangbo family.*

The explosions faded behind them. Whether the swordmasters were conserving their bombs or had exhausted them, he could not tell. He kept his guard raised, pain hammering through every step.

"Seems they are busy killing other rising talents," Hyeon Won-chang said after a while, his voice carrying guilt he could not quite conceal. "Makes me feel wretched."

"Why should we feel guilt when it is the Blood Flame Sect accumulating sins?" Shin So-bin replied.

Both were right. Both were wrong. It did not matter.

*Blood Flame Sect. I will split your skulls every time I see you.*

---

"There they are!"

Hyeon Won-chang pointed. Yeon-shin had already sensed them.

Rising talents from various martial arts families, including the Hwangbo siblings. None appeared injured. They were climbing the mountain path with swift lightness technique, heading for the ranges above.

Lazy Flame Dragon spotted them first. He spread his arms wide, opium poppies clenched between his teeth.

"What a state! Your limbs are intact though -- that is something. I will get you miraculous medicine. I have that much authority, even if they gossip about me being illegitimate."

"Brother!" Hwangbo Myeong-rin pushed him aside.

She approached Yeon-shin. Frost in her eyes. Grime on her face. Her features were delicate -- would have been beautiful if not for the arrogance etched into every line.

"You mongrel. What is your identity? Those swordsmen clearly came targeting you. What grudge did you bring to this Gathering?"

Silence.

"Answer. Do I look amusing to you?"

Yeon-shin scanned the others. Family young masters, all of them. The minor sect rising talents had died or scattered.

He stepped forward on his aching foot. Stood face to face with Hwangbo Myeong-rin.

Their eyes met at a distance of inches. Her beauty, wrapped in malice, looked particularly ugly.

"You tried to use my martial sister as bait," he said. "At the Lotus Pavilion."

The quiet mountain path filled with tension, and no one spoke.

---
